NKJV.1: Now concerning the collection for the saints, as I have given orders to the churches of Galatia, so you must do also:
NKJV.2: On the first day of the week let each one of you lay something aside, storing up as he may prosper, that there be no collections when I come.
NKJV.3: And when I come, whomever you approve by your letters I will send to bear your gift to Jerusalem.
NKJV.4: But if it is fitting that I go also, they will go with me.
NKJV.5: Now I will come to you when I pass through Macedonia (for I am passing through Macedonia).
NKJV.6: And it may be that I will remain, or even spend the winter with you, that you may send me on my journey, wherever I go.
NKJV.7: For I do not wish to see you now on the way; but I hope to stay a while with you, if the Lord permits.
NKJV.8: But I will tarry in Ephesus until Pentecost.
NKJV.9: For a great and effective door has opened to me, and there are many adversaries.
NKJV.10: And if Timothy comes, see that he may be with you without fear; for he does the work of the Lord, as I also do.
NKJV.11: Therefore let no one despise him. But send him on his journey in peace, that he may come to me; for I am waiting for him with the brethren.
NKJV.12: Now concerning our brother Apollos, I strongly urged him to come to you with the brethren, but he was quite unwilling to come at this time; however, he will come when he has a convenient time.
NKJV.13: Watch, stand fast in the faith, be brave, be strong.
NKJV.14: Let all that you do be done with love.
NKJV.15: I urge you, brethren—you know the household of Stephanas, that it is the firstfruits of Achaia, and that they have devoted themselves to the ministry of the saints—
NKJV.16: that you also submit to such, and to everyone who works and labors with us.
NKJV.17: I am glad about the coming of Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus, for what was lacking on your part they supplied.
NKJV.18: For they refreshed my spirit and yours. Therefore acknowledge such men.
NKJV.19: The churches of Asia greet you. Aquila and Priscilla greet you heartily in the Lord, with the church that is in their house.
NKJV.20: All the brethren greet you.  Greet one another with a holy kiss.
NKJV.21: The salutation with my own hand—Paul’s.
NKJV.22: If anyone does not love the Lord Jesus Christ, let him be accursed. O Lord, come!
NKJV.23: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
NKJV.24: My love be with you all in Christ Jesus. Amen.

ESV.1: Now concerning  the collection for the saints: as I directed the churches of Galatia, so you also are to do.
ESV.2: On the first day of every week, each of you is to put something aside and store it up, as he may prosper, so that there will be no collecting when I come.
ESV.3: And when I arrive, I will send those whom you accredit by letter to carry your gift to Jerusalem.
ESV.4: If it seems advisable that I should go also, they will accompany me.
ESV.5: I will visit you after passing through Macedonia, for I intend to pass through Macedonia,
ESV.6: and perhaps I will stay with you or even spend the winter, so that you may help me on my journey, wherever I go.
ESV.7: For I do not want to see you now just in passing. I hope to spend some time with you, if the Lord permits.
ESV.8: But I will stay in Ephesus until Pentecost,
ESV.9: for a wide door for effective work has opened to me, and there are many adversaries.
ESV.10: When Timothy comes, see that you put him at ease among you, for he is doing the work of the Lord, as I am.
ESV.11: So let no one despise him. Help him on his way in peace, that he may return to me, for I am expecting him with the brothers.
ESV.12: Now concerning our brother Apollos, I strongly urged him to visit you with the other brothers, but it was not at all his will  to come now. He will come when he has opportunity.
ESV.13: Be watchful, stand firm in the faith, act like men, be strong.
ESV.14: Let all that you do be done in love.
ESV.15: Now I urge you, brothers  — you know that the household  of Stephanas were the first converts in Achaia, and that they have devoted themselves to the service of the saints —
ESV.16: be subject to such as these, and to every fellow worker and laborer.
ESV.17: I rejoice at the coming of Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus, because they have made up for your absence,
ESV.18: for they refreshed my spirit as well as yours. Give recognition to such people.
ESV.19: The churches of Asia send you greetings. Aquila and Prisca, together with the church in their house, send you hearty greetings in the Lord.
ESV.20: All the brothers send you greetings. Greet one another with a holy kiss.
ESV.21: I, Paul, write this greeting with my own hand.
ESV.22: If anyone has no love for the Lord, let him be accursed. Our Lord, come!
ESV.23: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
ESV.24: My love be with you all in Christ Jesus. Amen.

NASB.1: Now concerning the collection for the saints, as I directed the churches of Galatia, so do you also.
NASB.2: On the first day of every week each one of you is to put aside and save, as he may prosper, so that no collections be made when I come.
NASB.3: When I arrive, whomever you may approve, I will send them with letters to carry your gift to Jerusalem;
NASB.4: and if it is fitting for me to go also, they will go with me.
NASB.5: But I will come to you after I go through Macedonia, for I am going through Macedonia;
NASB.6: and perhaps I will stay with you, or even spend the winter, so that you may send me on my way wherever I may go.
NASB.7: For I do not wish to see you now [just] in passing; for I hope to remain with you for some time, if the Lord permits.
NASB.8: But I will remain in Ephesus until Pentecost;
NASB.9: for a wide door for effective [service] has opened to me, and there are many adversaries.
NASB.10: Now if Timothy comes, see that he is with you without cause to be afraid, for he is doing the Lord's work, as I also am.
NASB.11: So let no one despise him. But send him on his way in peace, so that he may come to me; for I expect him with the brethren.
NASB.12: But concerning Apollos our brother, I encouraged him greatly to come to you with the brethren; and it was not at all [his] desire to come now, but he will come when he has opportunity.
NASB.13: Be on the alert, stand firm in the faith, act like men, be strong.
NASB.14: Let all that you do be done in love.
NASB.15: Now I urge you, brethren (you know the household of Stephanas, that they were the first fruits of Achaia, and that they have devoted themselves for ministry to the saints),
NASB.16: that you also be in subjection to such men and to everyone who helps in the work and labors.
NASB.17: I rejoice over the coming of Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus, because they have supplied what was lacking on your part.
NASB.18: For they have refreshed my spirit and yours. Therefore acknowledge such men.
NASB.19: The churches of Asia greet you. Aquila and Prisca greet you heartily in the Lord, with the church that is in their house.
NASB.20: All the brethren greet you. Greet one another with a holy kiss.
NASB.21: The greeting is in my own hand-- Paul.
NASB.22: If anyone does not love the Lord, he is to be accursed. Maranatha.
NASB.23: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
NASB.24: My love be with you all in Christ Jesus. Amen.

NLT.1: Now regarding your question about the money being collected for God’s people in Jerusalem. You should follow the same procedure I gave to the churches in Galatia.
NLT.2: On the first day of each week, you should each put aside a portion of the money you have earned. Don’t wait until I get there and then try to collect it all at once.
NLT.3: When I come, I will write letters of recommendation for the messengers you choose to deliver your gift to Jerusalem.
NLT.4: And if it seems appropriate for me to go along, they can travel with me.
NLT.5: I am coming to visit you after I have been to Macedonia, for I am planning to travel through Macedonia.
NLT.6: Perhaps I will stay awhile with you, possibly all winter, and then you can send me on my way to my next destination.
NLT.7: This time I don’t want to make just a short visit and then go right on. I want to come and stay awhile, if the Lord will let me.
NLT.8: In the meantime, I will be staying here at Ephesus until the Festival of Pentecost.
NLT.9: There is a wide-open door for a great work here, although many oppose me.
NLT.10: When Timothy comes, don’t intimidate him. He is doing the Lord’s work, just as I am.
NLT.11: Don’t let anyone treat him with contempt. Send him on his way with your blessing when he returns to me. I expect him to come with the other believers.
NLT.12: Now about our brother Apollos — I urged him to visit you with the other believers, but he was not willing to go right now. He will see you later when he has the opportunity.
NLT.13: Be on guard. Stand firm in the faith. Be courageous. Be strong.
NLT.15: You know that Stephanas and his household were the first of the harvest of believers in Greece, and they are spending their lives in service to God’s people. I urge you, dear brothers and sisters,
NLT.16: to submit to them and others like them who serve with such devotion.
NLT.17: I am very glad that Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus have come here. They have been providing the help you weren’t here to give me.
NLT.18: They have been a wonderful encouragement to me, as they have been to you. You must show your appreciation to all who serve so well.
NLT.19: The churches here in the province of Asia send greetings in the Lord, as do Aquila and Priscilla and all the others who gather in their home for church meetings.
NLT.20: All the brothers and sisters here send greetings to you. Greet each other with a sacred kiss.
NLT.21: HERE IS MY GREETING IN MY OWN HANDWRITING — PAUL.
NLT.22: If anyone does not love the Lord, that person is cursed. Our Lord, come!
NLT.23: May the grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
NLT.24: My love to all of you in Christ Jesus.

WEB.1: Now concerning the collection for the saints, as I commanded the assemblies of Galatia, you do likewise.
WEB.2: On the first day of the week, let each one of you save, as he may prosper, that no collections be made when I come.
WEB.3: When I arrive, I will send whoever you approve with letters to carry your gracious gift to Jerusalem.
WEB.4: If it is appropriate for me to go also, they will go with me.
WEB.5: But I will come to you when I have passed through Macedonia, for I am passing through Macedonia.
WEB.6: But with you it may be that I will stay, or even winter, that you may send me on my journey wherever I go.
WEB.7: For I do not wish to see you now in passing, but I hope to stay a while with you, if the Lord permits.
WEB.8: But I will stay at Ephesus until Pentecost,
WEB.9: for a great and effective door has opened to me, and there are many adversaries.
WEB.10: Now if Timothy comes, see that he is with you without fear, for he does the work of the Lord, as I also do.
WEB.11: Therefore let no one despise him. But set him forward on his journey in peace, that he may come to me; for I expect him with the brothers.
WEB.12: Now concerning Apollos, the brother, I strongly urged him to come to you with the brothers; and it was not at all his desire to come now; but he will come when he has an opportunity.
WEB.13: Watch! Stand firm in the faith! Be courageous! Be strong!
WEB.14: Let all that you do be done in love.
WEB.15: Now I beg you, brothers (you know the house of Stephanas, that it is the first fruits of Achaia, and that they have set themselves to serve the saints),
WEB.16: that you also be in subjection to such, and to everyone who helps in the work and labors.
WEB.17: I rejoice at the coming of Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus; for that which was lacking on your part, they supplied.
WEB.18: For they refreshed my spirit and yours. Therefore acknowledge those who are like that.
WEB.19: The assemblies of Asia greet you. Aquila and Priscilla greet you much in the Lord, together with the assembly that is in their house.
WEB.20: All the brothers greet you. Greet one another with a holy kiss.
WEB.21: This greeting is by me, Paul, with my own hand.
WEB.22: If any man doesn’t love the Lord Jesus Christ, let him be accursed.  Come, Lord!
WEB.23: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
WEB.24: My love to all of you in Christ Jesus. Amen.

CSB17.1: Now about the collection <sup>ⓓ</sup> for the saints: Do the same as I instructed the Galatian <sup>ⓔ</sup> churches.
CSB17.2: On the first day of the week, <sup>ⓕ</sup> each of you is to set something aside and save in keeping with how he is prospering, so that no collections will need to be made when I come. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.3: When I arrive, I will send with letters <sup>ⓗ</sup> those you recommend to carry your gift to Jerusalem. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.4: If it is suitable for me to go as well, they will travel with me.
CSB17.5: I will come to you after I pass through Macedonia <sup>ⓙ</sup>  — for I will be traveling through Macedonia —
CSB17.6: and perhaps I will remain with you or even spend the winter, so that you may send me on my way wherever I go.
CSB17.7: I don’t want to see you now just in passing, since I hope to spend some time with you, if the Lord allows. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.8: But I will stay in Ephesus <sup>ⓛ</sup> until Pentecost, <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.9: because a wide door <sup>ⓝ</sup> for effective ministry has opened for me <sup>[1]</sup>  — yet many oppose me.
CSB17.10: If Timothy comes, see that he has nothing to fear while with you, because he is doing the Lord’s work, <sup>ⓐ</sup> just as I am.
CSB17.11: So let no one look down <sup>ⓑ</sup> on him. Send him on his way in peace so that he can come to me, because I am expecting him with the brothers.
CSB17.12: Now about our brother Apollos: <sup>ⓒ</sup> I strongly urged him to come to you with the brothers, but he was not at all willing to come now. However, he will come when he has an opportunity.
CSB17.13: Be alert, stand firm in the faith, <sup>ⓓ</sup> be courageous, <sup>[2]</sup> be strong.
CSB17.15: Brothers and sisters, you know the household of Stephanas: They are the firstfruits <sup>ⓕ</sup> of Achaia <sup>ⓖ</sup> and have devoted themselves to serving the saints. I urge you
CSB17.16: also to submit to such people, and to everyone who works and labors with them.
CSB17.17: I am delighted to have Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus present, because these men have made up for your absence.
CSB17.18: For they have refreshed my spirit <sup>ⓗ</sup> and yours. Therefore recognize <sup>ⓘ</sup> such people.
CSB17.19: The churches of Asia <sup>ⓙ</sup> send you greetings. <sup>ⓚ</sup> Aquila and Priscilla <sup>ⓛ</sup> send you greetings warmly in the Lord, along with the church that meets in their home. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.20: All the brothers and sisters send you greetings. Greet one another with a holy kiss. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.21: This greeting is in my own hand <sup>ⓐ</sup>  — Paul.
CSB17.22: If anyone does not love the Lord, a curse be on him. Our Lord, come! <sup>[3]</sup>
CSB17.23: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.24: My love be with all of you in Christ Jesus.

AMP.1: Now concerning the money collected for [the relief of] the saints [in Jerusalem], you are to do the same as I directed the churches of Galatia to do.
AMP.2: On the first day of every week each one of you is to put something aside, in proportion to his prosperity, and save it so that no collections [will need to] be made when I come.
AMP.3: When I arrive, I will send whomever you approve with letters [of authorization] to take your gift [of charity and love] to Jerusalem;
AMP.4: and if it is fitting for me to go too, they will accompany me.
AMP.5: I will visit you after I go through Macedonia, for I am only passing through Macedonia;
AMP.6: but it may be that I will stay with you [for a while], or even spend the winter, so that you may send me on my way to wherever I may go afterward.
AMP.7: For I do not wish to see you right now just in passing, but I hope to remain with you for some time [later on], if the Lord permits.
AMP.8: But I will stay in Ephesus until Pentecost,
AMP.9: because a wide door for effective service has opened to me [in Ephesus, a very promising opportunity], and there are many adversaries.
AMP.10: If Timothy comes, see to it that [you put him at ease, so that] he has nothing to fear in regard to you, for he is [devotedly] doing the Lord's work, just as I am.
AMP.11: So allow no one to treat him with disdain [as if he were inconsequential]. But send him off [cordially, and speed him on his way] in peace, so that he may come to me, for I am expecting him [to come along] with the other brothers.
AMP.12: As for our brother Apollos, I have strongly encouraged him to visit you with the other brothers. It was not at all his desire to come now, but he will come when he has the opportunity.
AMP.13: Be on guard; stand firm in your faith [in God, respecting His precepts and keeping your doctrine sound]. Act like [mature] men and be courageous; be strong.
AMP.14: Let everything you do be done in love [motivated and inspired by God's love for us].
AMP.15: Brothers and sisters, you know that those of the household of Stephanas were the first converts in Achaia, and that they have devoted themselves for ministry to God's people—now I urge you
AMP.16: to be subject to such leaders [treating them with courtesy and respect], and to everyone who helps in the work and labors [for the benefit of yourselves and the church].
AMP.17: I rejoice because Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus have arrived, for they have made up for your absence.
AMP.18: They have refreshed my spirit as well as yours. So fully acknowledge such men and deeply appreciate them.
AMP.19: The churches of Asia send you their greetings. Aquila and Prisca, together with the church [that meets] in their house, send you their warm greetings in the Lord.
AMP.20: All the believers greet you. Greet one another with a holy kiss.
AMP.21: This greeting is in my own hand—Paul.
AMP.22: If anyone does not love the Lord [does not obey and respect and believe in Jesus Christ and His message], he is to be accursed. Maranatha (O our Lord, come)!
AMP.23: The grace of our Lord Jesus [His unmerited favor, His spiritual blessing, His profound mercy] be with you.
AMP.24: My love be with all of you in Christ Jesus. Amen.

MSG.1: Regarding the relief offering for poor Christians that is being collected, you get the same instructions I gave the churches in Galatia.
MSG.2: Every Sunday each of you make an offering and put it in safekeeping. Be as generous as you can. When I get there you'll have it ready, and I won't have to make a special appeal.
MSG.3: Then after I arrive, I'll write letters authorizing whomever you delegate, and send them off to Jerusalem to deliver your gift.
MSG.4: If you think it best that I go along, I'll be glad to travel with them.
MSG.5: I plan to visit you after passing through northern Greece. I won't be staying long there,
MSG.6: but maybe I can stay awhile with you—maybe even spend the winter? Then you could give me a good send-off, wherever I may be headed next.
MSG.7: I don't want to just drop by in between other "primary" destinations. I want a good, long, leisurely visit. If the Master agrees, we'll have it!
MSG.8: For the present, I'm staying right here in Ephesus.
MSG.9: A huge door of opportunity for good work has opened up here. (There is also mushrooming opposition.)
MSG.10: If Timothy shows up, take good care of him. Make him feel completely at home among you. He works so hard for the Master, just as I do.
MSG.11: Don't let anyone disparage him. After a while, send him on to me with your blessing. Tell him I'm expecting him, and any friends he has with him.
MSG.12: About our friend Apollos, I've done my best to get him to pay you a visit, but haven't talked him into it yet. He doesn't think this is the right time. But there will be a "right time."
MSG.13: Keep your eyes open, hold tight to your convictions, give it all you've got, be resolute,
MSG.15: Would you do me a favor, friends, and give special recognition to the family of Stephanas? You know, they were among the first converts in Greece, and they've put themselves out, serving Christians ever since then. I want you to
MSG.16: honor and look up to people like that: companions and workers who show us how to do it, giving us something to aspire to.
MSG.17: I want you to know how delighted I am to have Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus here with me. They partially make up for your absence!
MSG.18: They've refreshed me by keeping me in touch with you. Be proud that you have people like this among you.
MSG.19: The churches here in western Asia send greetings. Aquila, Priscilla, and the church that meets in their house say hello.
MSG.20: All the friends here say hello. Pass the greetings around with holy embraces!
MSG.21: And I, Paul—in my own handwriting!—send you my regards.
MSG.22: If anyone won't love the Master, throw him out. Make room for the Master!
MSG.23: Our Master Jesus has his arms wide open for you.
MSG.24: And I love all of you in the Messiah, in Jesus.

NIV.1:
The Collection for God’s People
Now about the collection for God’s people: Do what I told the Galatian churches to do.
NIV.2: On the first day of every week, each one of you should set aside a sum of money in keeping with his income, saving it up, so that when I come no collections will have to be made.
NIV.3: Then, when I arrive, I will give letters of introduction to the men you approve and send them with your gift to Jerusalem.
NIV.4: If it seems advisable for me to go also, they will accompany me.
NIV.5:
Personal Requests
After I go through Macedonia, I will come to you—for I will be going through Macedonia.
NIV.6: Perhaps I will stay with you awhile, or even spend the winter, so that you can help me on my journey, wherever I go.
NIV.7: I do not want to see you now and make only a passing visit; I hope to spend some time with you, if the Lord permits.
NIV.8: But I will stay on at Ephesus until Pentecost,
NIV.9: because a great door for effective work has opened to me, and there are many who oppose me.
NIV.10: If Timothy comes, see to it that he has nothing to fear while he is with you, for he is carrying on the work of the Lord, just as I am.
NIV.11: No one, then, should refuse to accept him. Send him on his way in peace so that he may return to me. I am expecting him along with the brothers.
NIV.12: Now about our brother Apollos: I strongly urged him to go to you with the brothers. He was quite unwilling to go now, but he will go when he has the opportunity.
NIV.13: Be on your guard; stand firm in the faith; be men of courage; be strong.
NIV.15: You know that the household of Stephanas were the first converts in Achaia, and they have devoted themselves to the service of the saints. I urge you, brothers,
NIV.16: to submit to such as these and to everyone who joins in the work, and labors at it.
NIV.17: I was glad when Stephanas, Fortunatus and Achaicus arrived, because they have supplied what was lacking from you.
NIV.18: For they refreshed my spirit and yours also. Such men deserve recognition.
NIV.19:
Final Greetings
The churches in the province of Asia send you greetings. Aquila and Priscilla greet you warmly in the Lord, and so does the church that meets at their house.
NIV.20: All the brothers here send you greetings. Greet one another with a holy kiss.
NIV.21: I, Paul, write this greeting in my own hand.
NIV.22: If anyone does not love the Lord—a curse be on him. Come, O Lord!
NIV.23: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
NIV.24: My love to all of you in Christ Jesus. Amen.

NRSVCE.1: Now concerning the collection for the saints: you should follow the directions I gave to the churches of Galatia.
NRSVCE.2: On the first day of every week, each of you is to put aside and save whatever extra you earn, so that collections need not be taken when I come.
NRSVCE.3: And when I arrive, I will send any whom you approve with letters to take your gift to Jerusalem.
NRSVCE.4: If it seems advisable that I should go also, they will accompany me.
NRSVCE.5: I will visit you after passing through Macedonia — for I intend to pass through Macedonia —
NRSVCE.6: and perhaps I will stay with you or even spend the winter, so that you may send me on my way, wherever I go.
NRSVCE.7: I do not want to see you now just in passing, for I hope to spend some time with you, if the Lord permits.
NRSVCE.8: But I will stay in Ephesus until Pentecost,
NRSVCE.9: for a wide door for effective work has opened to me, and there are many adversaries.
NRSVCE.10: If Timothy comes, see that he has nothing to fear among you, for he is doing the work of the Lord just as I am;
NRSVCE.11: therefore let no one despise him. Send him on his way in peace, so that he may come to me; for I am expecting him with the brothers.
NRSVCE.12: Now concerning our brother Apollos, I strongly urged him to visit you with the other brothers, but he was not at all willing to come now. He will come when he has the opportunity.
NRSVCE.13: Keep alert, stand firm in your faith, be courageous, be strong.
NRSVCE.14: Let all that you do be done in love.
NRSVCE.15: Now, brothers and sisters, you know that members of the household of Stephanas were the first converts in Achaia, and they have devoted themselves to the service of the saints;
NRSVCE.16: I urge you to put yourselves at the service of such people, and of everyone who works and toils with them.
NRSVCE.17: I rejoice at the coming of Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus, because they have made up for your absence;
NRSVCE.18: for they refreshed my spirit as well as yours. So give recognition to such persons.
NRSVCE.19: The churches of Asia send greetings. Aquila and Prisca, together with the church in their house, greet you warmly in the Lord.
NRSVCE.20: All the brothers and sisters send greetings. Greet one another with a holy kiss.
NRSVCE.21: I, Paul, write this greeting with my own hand.
NRSVCE.22: Let anyone be accursed who has no love for the Lord. Our Lord, come!
NRSVCE.23: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
NRSVCE.24: My love be with all of you in Christ Jesus.

NET.1: With regard to the collection for the saints, please follow the directions that I gave to the churches of Galatia:
NET.2: On the first day of the week, each of you should set aside some income and save it to the extent that God has blessed you, so that a collection will not have to be made when I come.
NET.3: Then, when I arrive, I will send those whom you approve with letters of explanation to carry your gift to Jerusalem.
NET.4: And if it seems advisable that I should go also, they will go with me.
NET.5: But I will come to you after I have gone through Macedonia — for I will be going through Macedonia —
NET.6: and perhaps I will stay with you, or even spend the winter, so that you can send me on my journey, wherever I go.
NET.7: For I do not want to see you now in passing, since I hope to spend some time with you, if the Lord allows.
NET.8: But I will stay in Ephesus until Pentecost,
NET.9: because a door of great opportunity stands wide open for me, but there are many opponents.
NET.10: Now if Timothy comes, see that he has nothing to fear among you, for he is doing the Lord’s work, as I am too.
NET.11: So then, let no one treat him with contempt. But send him on his way in peace so that he may come to me. For I am expecting him with the brothers.
NET.12: With regard to our brother Apollos: I strongly encouraged him to visit you with the other brothers, but it was simply not his intention to come now. He will come when he has the opportunity.
NET.13: Stay alert, stand firm in the faith, show courage, be strong.
NET.14: Everything you do should be done in love.
NET.15: Now, brothers and sisters, you know about the household of Stephanus, that as the first converts of Achaia, they devoted themselves to ministry for the saints. I urge you
NET.16: also to submit to people like this, and to everyone who cooperates in the work and labors hard.
NET.17: I was glad about the arrival of Stephanus, Fortunatus, and Achaicus because they have supplied the fellowship with you that I lacked.
NET.18: For they refreshed my spirit and yours. So then, recognize people like this.
NET.19: The churches in the province of Asia send greetings to you. Aquila and Prisca greet you warmly in the Lord, with the church that meets in their house.
NET.20: All the brothers and sisters send greetings. Greet one another with a holy kiss.
NET.21: I, Paul, send this greeting with my own hand.
NET.22: Let anyone who has no love for the Lord be accursed. Our Lord, come!
NET.23: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
NET.24: My love be with all of you in Christ Jesus.
